name: Advanced IP Scanner – Last IP Range Scanned
categoryDescription: Registry artifacts for commonly abused tools with specific registry footprints: PsExec (temporary service key on target), FileZilla (FTP exfiltration), MEGASync (cloud exfiltration with autorun), Advanced IP Scanner (writes even as portable; operational history including scanned IP ranges), and NetSupport Manager (RMM abused as RAT). Includes artifact tips and companion file artifacts.
description: [Advanced IP Scanner] Operational history subkey. LastRangeUsed = most recent IP range scanned. IpRangesMruList = all ranges scanned; the first digit of each prefix is the scan count for that range. SearchMruList = individual IP addresses searched. locale = UI language.
forensic_value: LastRangeUsed directly shows what network segment was targeted. IpRangesMruList reveals the full scope of reconnaissance — scan counts expose priorities (e.g., scanning a SCADA subnet 12 times). SearchMruList shows specific targets of interest. locale value (e.g., 'ru', 'zh-CN') may indicate operator's native language. Per Hunt & Hackett: 'the first digit of each prefix in IpRangesMruList is the number of times that range was scanned.'
